forecast-period.text: A series of low pressure systems passing through the Bight will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week. A high pressure system is then forecast to move into the Bight from late Friday and extend a ridge across the state over the weekend.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers and isolated thunderstorms about the central west extending eastwards during the morning, contracting to the Southern Agricultural Area during the evening. Severe thunderstorms with gusty winds and large hail are possible about parts of the Flinders, Mid North, Riverland and Murraylands districts and southern parts of the North East Pastoral district during the late morning to afternoon period. Maximum temperatures average to above average, tending below average in the west. Fresh and gusty west to northwesterly winds, moderating in the evening but remaining fresh and gusty about the ranges.

Showers about the Southern Agricultural Area, possibly extending further north into the pastoral areas. Possible damaging winds about southern coastal districts, along with possible isolated thunderstorms. Possible small hail about the Lower South East. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh and gusty west to northwesterly winds, ahead of a west to southwesterly wind change in the evening.

Isolated light showers about southern coastal districts. Maximum temperatures below average. Gusty southwesterly winds about the southern coastal districts easing, tending south to southeasterly further north and west.

text.p: Isolated light showers about southern coastal districts and near the northern border. Maximum temperatures below average. South to southeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: less than 2 mm for parts of the North West Pastoral district but broadly 5-15 mm across the state, 15-30 mm possible about central and eastern districts and near the western coast of Eyre Peninsula. Local falls of 30-50 mm possible about Kangaroo Island, Mt Lofty Ranges and the Lower South East.
